what_it_does: Builds a human-readable diagnostic report for the Episodic Memory Codex integration by parsing Codex CLI version, feature flags, MCP server list, sessions directory presence, and hook trust state, then listing status and any remediation issues.
observations: Parses feature output by finding a line starting with the feature name and reading the last whitespace-separated column as true or false.
Parses the MCP list by looking for a line starting with 'episodic-memory ' and checking for the substring ' enabled'.
Maps a CodexHookTrustState enum to user-facing trust instructions via a switch statement.
Collects issues into an array and marks the report ok only when no issues exist.
Formats the final report as a plain-text string with headers, status lines, and a bulleted issue list.
standouts: Relies entirely on parsing human-readable Codex CLI output rather than structured data.
Trust-state handling embeds specific user actions such as opening /hooks and pressing t to trust.
conventions: Uses TypeScript explicit return types and local const bindings.
Uses early-return guards and array join for string building.
Separates pure parsing helpers from the main report builder.
concerns: Text parsing is brittle: feature state depends on the last column being exactly 'true' or 'false', and MCP detection depends on a substring ' enabled'.
Hook trust issue messages partially duplicate the formatted trust explanation.
No fallback if Codex output contains extra whitespace or renamed labels.
notes: A diagnostic formatter that turns raw Codex CLI state into a plain-text health report with actionable fix instructions.
questions: What exact format do codex version, features, and mcp list produce, and how stable is it?
How is CodexHookTrustState computed and what does 'unknown' mean in practice?
What consumes DoctorReport — a CLI command, a log file, or an IDE panel?
purpose_vs_actual: The file diagnoses Codex/Episodic Memory integration readiness and produces a remediation report, which matches its content.
intention_signals: It tries to give a non-technical user a single plain-text checklist that shows whether Codex and the Episodic Memory plugin are correctly configured and, if not, what command or UI action to take next.
contribution: A self-contained diagnostic formatter that translates low-level Codex integration signals into a readable, actionable report with explicit remediation steps.
summary_for_embedding: This file implements a Codex doctor report builder that parses CLI outputs for version, feature flags, MCP server status, and hook trust to produce a plain-text integration health report.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to help users verify and fix Episodic Memory plugin setup by surfacing actionable diagnostic messages from Codex state.

symbols.does: Declares the input shape for building a doctor report.
symbols.description: Groups all raw Codex integration signals—CLI outputs, paths, directory existence, and hook trust state—into a single typed input for report generation.

symbols.does: Declares the output shape of a doctor report.
symbols.description: Represents the generated diagnostic result as a boolean ok flag plus the plain-text report string.

symbols.does: Parses a boolean feature flag from Codex features output.
symbols.description: Splits the features output into lines, finds the line starting with the requested feature name, and returns true or false based on the last whitespace-separated column, or undefined if missing or unrecognized.

symbols.does: Parses the Episodic Memory MCP server state from codex mcp list output.
symbols.description: Scans the MCP list for a line beginning with 'episodic-memory ' and returns enabled, disabled, or missing depending on whether ' enabled' appears.

symbols.does: Formats a hook trust enum value into a human-readable instruction.
symbols.description: Maps trusted, untrusted, modified, not_found, and unknown states to concise status text that tells the user how to verify or re-trust the hook.

symbols.does: Builds the full doctor report from inputs.
symbols.description: Parses all input signals, accumulates integration issues, renders status lines for version, features, MCP, paths, and hook trust, and returns a DoctorReport with ok set when no issues are found.
